+# or visit www.oracle.com if you need additional information or have any
+# questions.

+.TEST_FOR_NON_GNUMAKE:sh=echo You are not using GNU Make/gmake, this is a requirement. Check your path. 1>&2 && exit 1
+
+ifeq ($(filter /%, $(lastword $(MAKEFILE_LIST))),)
+  makefile_path := $(CURDIR)/$(strip $(lastword $(MAKEFILE_LIST)))
+else
+  makefile_path := $(lastword $(MAKEFILE_LIST))
+endif
+TOPDIR := $(strip $(patsubst %/, %, $(dir $(makefile_path))))
+
+include $(TOPDIR)/make/Main.gmk

@@ -0,0 +1,85 @@
+# javax.script Engine for JavaScript Based on V8
+
+This is a [V8](https://v8.dev/)-based [javax.script](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/25/docs/api/java.scripting/javax/script/package-summary.html) engine for Java.
+
+## Prerequisites to build
+
+* `git`
+* `make`
+* **JDK 25** (set `JAVA_HOME` env. var to that JDK)
+* Typical build utilities: `bash`, `tar`, `zip`, `curl` (often already present)
+* **Python 3** (required by V8 tooling)
+* C/C++ toolchain for your OS
+  * **Linux**: `gcc/g++` or `clang`, plus common build packages (varies by distro)
+  * **macOS**: Xcode Command Line Tools (`xcode-select --install`)
+
+## Quick Checks
+
+```bash
+git --version
+make --version
+python3 --version
+${JAVA_HOME}/bin/java -version
+```
+
+## Build and Test
+
+* Clone the depot_tools into a directory outside this repo. This provides key tools used to fetch and build V8 (gclient, fetch, gn, ninja...).
+
+  ```sh
+  git clone https://chromium.googlesource.com/chromium/tools/depot_tools
+  ```
+
+* Set the `depot_tools` repo directory in `PATH`
+
+  ```sh
+  export PATH="<your-depot-tools-dir>:$PATH"
+  ```
+
+* To fetch/sync the V8 sources, specifically version 14.4.221 and dependencies:
+
+  ```sh
+  make get-v8
+  ```
+
+* To do a clean build:
+
+  ```sh
+  make clean all
+  ```
+
+* (Optional) To run [jtreg](https://openjdk.org/jtreg/) tests:
+
+  ```sh
+  export JT_HOME=<your_jtreg_home_dir>
+  make test
+  ```
+
+* To run the interactive shell:
+
+  ```sh
+  bash jjs.sh
+  v8> java.lang.System.out.println("hello world")
+  hello world
+  v8> java.lang.System.exit(0)
+  ```
+
+* There are samples in the `samples` directory. To run a sample script:
+
+  ```sh
+  bash jjs.sh samples/helloworld.js
+  ```
+
+* There are also Java samples. To run a Java sample:
+
+  ```sh
+  bash run-java-sample.sh samples/HelloWorld.java
+  ```
+
+* To create a distribution bundle:
+
+  ```sh
+  make dist bundles
+  ```
+
+  The resulting .tar.gz bundle is written to `build/<platform-specific-dir>/bundles/`.

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+#
+# Copyright (c) 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates. All rights reserved.
+#
+# Redistribution and use in source and binary forms, with or without
+# modification, are permitted provided that the following conditions
+# are met:
+#
+#   - Redistributions of source code must retain the above copyright
+#     notice, this list of conditions and the following disclaimer.
+#
+#   - Redistributions in binary form must reproduce the above copyright
+#     notice, this list of conditions and the following disclaimer in the
+#     documentation and/or other materials provided with the distribution.
+#
+#   - Neither the name of Oracle nor the names of its
+#     contributors may be used to endorse or promote products derived
+#     from this software without specific prior written permission.
+#
+# THIS SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED BY THE COPYRIGHT HOLDERS AND CONTRIBUTORS "AS
+# IS" AND ANY EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTIES, INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO,
+# THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR
+# PURPOSE ARE DISCLAIMED.  IN NO EVENT SHALL THE COPYRIGHT OWNER OR
+# CONTRIBUTORS BE LIABLE FOR ANY DIRECT, INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL,
+# EXEMPLARY, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES (INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO,
+# PROCUREMENT OF SUBSTITUTE GOODS OR SERVICES; LOSS OF USE, DATA, OR
+# PROFITS; OR BUSINESS INTERRUPTION) HOWEVER CAUSED AND ON ANY THEORY OF
+# LIABILITY, WHETHER IN CONTRACT, STRICT LIABILITY, OR TORT (INCLUDING
+# NEGLIGENCE OR OTHERWISE) ARISING IN ANY WAY OUT OF THE USE OF THIS
+# SOFTWARE, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGE.
+
+mydir="$(cd $(dirname ${BASH_SOURCE[0]}) > /dev/null && pwd)"
+
+java \
+    --module-path "${mydir}/../lib/org.openjdk.engine.javascript.jar" \
+    --add-modules org.openjdk.engine.javascript \
+    --enable-native-access=org.openjdk.engine.javascript \
+    org.openjdk.engine.javascript.Main "$@"

@@ -0,0 +1,27 @@
+# This is the Detroit javascript engine for Java.
+
+Note: You need to have JDK 25 on the PATH:
+
+```bash
+export PATH=/path/to/jdk-25:$PATH"
+```
+
+For an interactive shell, please run:
+
+```bash
+sh bin/jjs.sh
+```
+
+To run .js files:
+
+```bash
+sh bin/jjs.sh <script-file>
+```
+
+To run java source files that use detroit engine:
+
+```bash
+sh bin/run.sh <.java file>
+```
+
+The "samples" folder has .js and .java samples.

+#!/bin/bash
+#
+# Copyright (c) 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates. All rights reserved.
+#
+# Redistribution and use in source and binary forms, with or without
+# modification, are permitted provided that the following conditions
+# are met:
+#
+#   - Redistributions of source code must retain the above copyright
+#     notice, this list of conditions and the following disclaimer.
+#
+#   - Redistributions in binary form must reproduce the above copyright
+#     notice, this list of conditions and the following disclaimer in the
+#     documentation and/or other materials provided with the distribution.
+#
+#   - Neither the name of Oracle nor the names of its
+#     contributors may be used to endorse or promote products derived
+#     from this software without specific prior written permission.
+#
+# THIS SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED BY THE COPYRIGHT HOLDERS AND CONTRIBUTORS "AS
+# IS" AND ANY EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTIES, INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO,
+# THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR
+# PURPOSE ARE DISCLAIMED.  IN NO EVENT SHALL THE COPYRIGHT OWNER OR
+# CONTRIBUTORS BE LIABLE FOR ANY DIRECT, INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL,
+# EXEMPLARY, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES (INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO,
+# PROCUREMENT OF SUBSTITUTE GOODS OR SERVICES; LOSS OF USE, DATA, OR
+# PROFITS; OR BUSINESS INTERRUPTION) HOWEVER CAUSED AND ON ANY THEORY OF
+# LIABILITY, WHETHER IN CONTRACT, STRICT LIABILITY, OR TORT (INCLUDING
+# NEGLIGENCE OR OTHERWISE) ARISING IN ANY WAY OUT OF THE USE OF THIS
+# SOFTWARE, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGE.
+
+SCRIPTDIR="$(dirname ${BASH_SOURCE[0]})"
+
+builds=($(ls ${SCRIPTDIR}/build/))
+
+if [ ${#builds[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
+  echo "No build found. Run 'make dist' first"
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+if [ ${#builds[@]} -gt 1 ]; then
+  echo "Multiple builds found, picking '${builds[0]}'"
+fi
+
+build=${builds[0]} # just pick the first one
+lib=${SCRIPTDIR}/build/$build/dist/org.openjdk.engine.javascript/lib
+
+java \
+  --module-path $lib \
+  --add-modules org.openjdk.engine.javascript \
+  --enable-native-access=org.openjdk.engine.javascript \
+  org.openjdk.engine.javascript.Main -cp .  -l v8-no-java "$@"

+  exit 1

+  echo "Multiple builds found, picking '${builds[0]}'"

+  -Djvmv8.dump.generated.script=true \
+  -Djvmv8.debug=true \

+== builtins

+== License
+A. HISTORY OF THE SOFTWARE
+==========================
+
+Python was created in the early 1990s by Guido van Rossum at Stichting
+Mathematisch Centrum (CWI, see http://www.cwi.nl) in the Netherlands
+as a successor of a language called ABC.  Guido remains Python's
+principal author, although it includes many contributions from others.
+
+In 1995, Guido continued his work on Python at the Corporation for
+National Research Initiatives (CNRI, see http://www.cnri.reston.va.us)
+in Reston, Virginia where he released several versions of the
+software.
+
+In May 2000, Guido and the Python core development team moved to
+BeOpen.com to form the BeOpen PythonLabs team.  In October of the same
+year, the PythonLabs team moved to Digital Creations, which became
+Zope Corporation.  In 2001, the Python Software Foundation (PSF, see
+https://www.python.org/psf/) was formed, a non-profit organization
+created specifically to own Python-related Intellectual Property.
+Zope Corporation was a sponsoring member of the PSF.
+
+All Python releases are Open Source (see http://www.opensource.org for
+the Open Source Definition).  Historically, most, but not all, Python
+releases have also been GPL-compatible; the table below summarizes
+the various releases.
+
+    Release         Derived     Year        Owner       GPL-
+                    from                                compatible? (1)
+
+    0.9.0 thru 1.2              1991-1995   CWI         yes
+    1.3 thru 1.5.2  1.2         1995-1999   CNRI        yes
+    1.6             1.5.2       2000        CNRI        no
+    2.0             1.6         2000        BeOpen.com  no
+    1.6.1           1.6         2001        CNRI        yes (2)
+    2.1             2.0+1.6.1   2001        PSF         no
+    2.0.1           2.0+1.6.1   2001        PSF         yes
+    2.1.1           2.1+2.0.1   2001        PSF         yes
+    2.1.2           2.1.1       2002        PSF         yes
+    2.1.3           2.1.2       2002        PSF         yes
+    2.2 and above   2.1.1       2001-now    PSF         yes
+
+Footnotes:
+
+(1) GPL-compatible doesn't mean that we're distributing Python under
+    the GPL.  All Python licenses, unlike the GPL, let you distribute
+    a modified version without making your changes open source.  The
+    GPL-compatible licenses make it possible to combine Python with
+    other software that is released under the GPL; the others don't.
+
+(2) According to Richard Stallman, 1.6.1 is not GPL-compatible,
+    because its license has a choice of law clause.  According to
+    CNRI, however, Stallman's lawyer has told CNRI's lawyer that 1.6.1
+    is "not incompatible" with the GPL.
+
+Thanks to the many outside volunteers who have worked under Guido's
+direction to make these releases possible.

+

+
+BUNDLE_DIST := $(BUNDLES_DIR)/org.openjdk.engine.javascript-$(DETROIT_BUILD_VER)-$(TARGET_PLATFORM).tar.gz
+

+all: bundles

+#
+# Copyright (c) 2025, Oracle and/or its affiliates. All rights reserved.
+# DO NOT ALTER OR REMOVE COPYRIGHT NOTICES OR THIS FILE HEADER.
+#
+# This code is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it
+# under the terms of the GNU General Public License version 2 only, as
+# published by the Free Software Foundation.  Oracle designates this
+# particular file as subject to the "Classpath" exception as provided
+# by Oracle in the LICENSE file that accompanied this code.
+#
+# This code is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, but WITHOUT
+# ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of MERCHANTABILITY or
+# FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU General Public License
+# version 2 for more details (a copy is included in the LICENSE file that
+# accompanied this code).
+#
+# You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License version
+# 2 along with this work; if not, write to the Free Software Foundation,
+# Inc., 51 Franklin St, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA 02110-1301 USA.
+#
+# Please contact Oracle, 500 Oracle Parkway, Redwood Shores, CA 94065 USA
+# or visit www.oracle.com if you need additional information or have any
+# questions.
+#
+
+SHELL := /bin/bash
+
+EMPTY :=
+SPACE := $(EMPTY) $(EMPTY)
+DOLLAR := $$
+
+UNAME := $(shell uname)
+UNAME_m := $(shell uname -m)
+ifeq ($(UNAME),Darwin)
+    HOST_OS := macos
+    JDK_JNI_OS := darwin
+    BRAND := $(shell sysctl -n machdep.cpu.brand_string)
+
+    ifneq (,$(findstring Apple, $(BRAND)))
+        HOST_CPU := aarch64
+    else
+        HOST_CPU := x64
+    endif
+else
+    HOST_OS := linux
+    JDK_JNI_OS := linux
+    ifeq ($(UNAME_M),aarch64)
+        HOST_CPU := aarch64
+    else
+        HOST_CPU := x64
+    endif
+endif
+
+HOST_PLATFORM := $(HOST_OS)-$(HOST_CPU)
+
+TARGET_OS := $(HOST_OS)
+ifeq ($(TARGET_CPU),)
+    TARGET_CPU := $(HOST_CPU)
+endif
+
+TARGET_PLATFORM := $(TARGET_OS)-$(TARGET_CPU)
+
+ifeq ($(HOST_CPU), x64)
+    V8_HOST_CPU := x64
+else ifeq ($(HOST_CPU), aarch64)
+    V8_HOST_CPU := arm64
+else
+    $(error Unknown HOST_CPU: $(HOST_CPU))
+endif
+
+ifeq ($(TARGET_CPU), x64)
+    V8_TARGET_CPU := x64
+    ifeq ($(TARGET_OS), macos)
+        CLANG_TARGET := x86_64-apple-darwin25.1.0
+    else
+        CLANG_TARGET := x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
+    endif
+else ifeq ($(TARGET_CPU), aarch64)
+    V8_TARGET_CPU := arm64
+    CLANG_TARGET=aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
+else
+    $(error "Unsupported target cpu: $(TARGET_CPU)")
+endif
+
+ifneq ($(HOST_OS), $(TARGET_OS))
+    $(error "Cross compiling across operating systems not supported")
+endif
+
+ifeq ($(TARGET_OS), macos)
+    LIB_PREFIX := lib
+    LIB_SUFFIX := .dylib
+else ifeq ($(TARGET_OS), linux)
+    LIB_PREFIX := lib
+    LIB_SUFFIX := .so
+else
+    $(error "OS not supported yet")
+endif
+
+
+####
+#
+# Tools
+#
+
+AWK := awk
+CAT := cat
+CD := cd
+CP := cp
+CUT := cut
+ECHO := echo
+FIND := find
+GIT := git
+LN := ln
+MKDIR := mkdir
+MV := mv
+NINJA := ninja
+RM := rm
+SED := sed
+TAR := tar
+TOUCH := touch
+
+ifeq ($(TARGET_OS), macos)
+    INSTALL_NAME_TOOL := install_name_tool
+endif
+
+
+####
+#
+# Build directory information
+#
+
+BUILD_DIR := $(TOPDIR)/build/$(TARGET_PLATFORM)
+
+MARKERS_DIR := $(BUILD_DIR)/make-support
+
+NATIVE_DIR := $(BUILD_DIR)/native
+
+
+####
+#
+# Version information
+#
+
+include $(TOPDIR)/conf/v8/version.conf
+
+ifneq ($(wildcard $(TOPDIR)/.git),)
+  GIT_REV_ONLY := $(shell $(CD) $(TOPDIR) && $(GIT) rev-parse HEAD)
+  GIT_REV_SUFFIX := $(if $(shell $(CD) $(TOPDIR) && $(GIT) status --porcelain),+)
+  GIT_REV := $(GIT_REV_ONLY)$(GIT_REV_SUFFIX)
+  GIT_REV_SHORT := $(shell $(ECHO) $(GIT_REV) | $(CUT) -c1-8)$(GIT_REV_SUFFIX)
+  GIT_REV_FILE := $(JVMV8_BUILD_DIR)/.git-rev
+endif
+
+DATE := $(shell date +%Y-%m-%d)
+DETROIT_BUILD_VER := $(DATE)$(if $(GIT_REV_SHORT),+$(GIT_REV_SHORT))
+
+
+####
+#
+# v8
+#
+
+V8_ROOT_DIR := $(TOPDIR)/v8
+V8_REPO := $(V8_ROOT_DIR)/v8
+V8_CONF_DIR := $(TOPDIR)/conf/v8
+V8_BUILD_DIR := $(BUILD_DIR)/v8/$(V8_TARGET_CPU).release
+
+V8_INCLUDE_DIRS := \
+    $(V8_REPO) \
+    $(V8_REPO)/include \
+    $(V8_REPO)/third_party/abseil-cpp \
+    $(V8_REPO)/third_party/fp16/src/include \
+    $(V8_BUILD_DIR)/gen \
+    $(V8_BUILD_DIR)/gen/include \
+    $(V8_REPO)/buildtools/third_party/libc++ \
+    $(V8_REPO)/third_party/libc++/src/include \
+    #
+
+V8_CXX := $(V8_REPO)/third_party/llvm-build/Release+Asserts/bin/clang++
+V8_DEFINES := \
+    -D_LIBCPP_HARDENING_MODE=_LIBCPP_HARDENING_MODE_EXTENSIVE \
+    #
+
+# Needed when building v8 debug?
+#V8_DEFINES += -DV8_ENABLE_CHECKS=ENABLED
+
+ifeq ($(TARGET_OS), linux)
+    ifeq ($(V8_TARGET_CPU),x64)
+        V8_SYSROOT=$(V8_REPO)/build/linux/debian_bullseye_amd64-sysroot
+    else ifeq ($(V8_TARGET_CPU),arm64)
+        V8_SYSROOT=$(V8_REPO)/build/linux/debian_bullseye_arm64-sysroot
+    else
+        $(error "Unknown V8_TARGET_CPU: $(V8_TARGET_CPU)")
+    endif
+endif
+
+
+####
+#
+# jvmv8
+#
+
+JVMV8_MODULE_NAME := org.openjdk.engine.javascript
+
+JVMV8_BUILD_DIR := $(BUILD_DIR)/jvmv8
+JVMV8_JAR_FILE_NAME := $(JVMV8_MODULE_NAME).jar
+JVMV8_JAR := $(JVMV8_BUILD_DIR)/jar/$(JVMV8_JAR_FILE_NAME)
+JVMV8_DOCS_DIR := $(JVMV8_BUILD_DIR)/docs
+
+JVMV8_LIB_FILE_NAME := $(LIB_PREFIX)jvmv8$(LIB_SUFFIX)
+JVMV8_LIB_DIR := $(JVMV8_BUILD_DIR)/lib
+JVMV8_LIB := $(JVMV8_LIB_DIR)/$(JVMV8_LIB_FILE_NAME)
+
+####
+#
+# Dist information
+#
+
+DIST_DIR := $(BUILD_DIR)/dist/org.openjdk.engine.javascript
+DIST_LIB_DIR := $(DIST_DIR)/lib
+DIST_JAR := $(DIST_LIB_DIR)/$(JVMV8_JAR_FILE_NAME)
+DIST_MODULE_PATH := $(DIST_JAR)
+
+# Java options needed to run detroit
+DIST_JAVA_OPTIONS := \
+    --module-path $(DIST_MODULE_PATH) \
+    --enable-native-access=$(JVMV8_MODULE_NAME) \
+    #

+#
+# Copyright (c) 2025, Oracle and/or its affiliates. All rights reserved.
+# DO NOT ALTER OR REMOVE COPYRIGHT NOTICES OR THIS FILE HEADER.
+#
+# This code is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it

+# published by the Free Software Foundation.  Oracle designates this
+# particular file as subject to the "Classpath" exception as provided
+# by Oracle in the LICENSE file that accompanied this code.
+#
+# This code is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, but WITHOUT
+# ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of MERCHANTABILITY or
+# FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU General Public License
+# version 2 for more details (a copy is included in the LICENSE file that
+# accompanied this code).
+#
+# You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License version
+# 2 along with this work; if not, write to the Free Software Foundation,
+# Inc., 51 Franklin St, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA 02110-1301 USA.
+#
+# Please contact Oracle, 500 Oracle Parkway, Redwood Shores, CA 94065 USA
+# or visit www.oracle.com if you need additional information or have any
+# questions.
+#
+
+include Common.gmk
+include SetupJava.gmk
+
+DOCS := $(shell find $(JVMV8_DOCS_DIR) -type f)
+
+SAMPLES_DIR := $(TOPDIR)/samples
+SAMPLES := $(filter-out $(SAMPLES_DIR)/failing,$(wildcard $(SAMPLES_DIR)/*))
+
+JMODS_DIR := $(BUILD_DIR)/jmods
+JMOD_FILE := $(JMODS_DIR)/$(JVMV8_MODULE_NAME).jmod
+JMODS_MARKER := $(MARKERS_DIR)/jmods.marker
+
+ifneq ($(GIT_REV_SHORT),)
+    DIST_GIT_REV_FILE := $(DIST_DIR)/.git-rev
+endif
+
+BIN_FILES := $(TOPDIR)/bin/jjs.sh $(TOPDIR)/bin/run.sh
+
+DIST_BIN_DIR := $(DIST_DIR)/bin
+DIST_DOCS_DIR := $(DIST_DIR)/docs
+DIST_JMODS_DIR := $(DIST_DIR)/jmods
+DIST_SAMPLES_DIR := $(DIST_DIR)/samples
+
+DIST_MARKER := $(MARKERS_DIR)/dist.marker
+
+$(JMODS_MARKER): $(JVMV8_JAR) $(JVMV8_LIB)
+	@$(MKDIR) -p $(@D)
+	@$(RM) -rf $(JMODS_DIR)
+	@$(MKDIR) -p $(JMODS_DIR)
+	@$(JMOD) --class-path $(JVMV8_JAR) \
+	    --libs $(JVMV8_LIB_DIR) \
+	    create \
+	    $(JMOD_FILE)
+	@$(TOUCH) $@
+
+$(DIST_MARKER): $(JMODS_MARKER) $(JVMV8_JAR) $(JVMV8_LIB) $(DOCS) $(SAMPLES)
+	@$(MKDIR) -p $(@D)
+	$(RM) -rf $(DIST_DIR)
+	$(MKDIR) -p $(DIST_DIR)
+ifneq ($(DIST_GIT_REV_FILE),)
+	@echo "$(GIT_REV_SHORT)" > $(DIST_GIT_REV_FILE)
+endif
+	$(MKDIR) -p $(DIST_BIN_DIR)
+	$(CP) $(TOPDIR)/ADDITIONAL_LICENSE_INFO $(DIST_DIR)
+	$(CP) $(TOPDIR)/LICENSE $(DIST_DIR)
+	$(CP) -r $(TOPDIR)/legal $(DIST_DIR)
+	$(CP) $(TOPDIR)/doc/README.md $(DIST_DIR)
+	$(CP) $(BIN_FILES) $(DIST_BIN_DIR)
+	$(CP) -r $(JMODS_DIR) $(DIST_JMODS_DIR)
+	$(MKDIR) -p $(DIST_LIB_DIR)
+	$(CP) -r $(JVMV8_LIB) $(DIST_LIB_DIR)
+	$(MKDIR) -p $(dir $(DIST_JAR))
+	$(CP) $(JVMV8_JAR) $(DIST_JAR)
+	$(CP) -r $(JVMV8_DOCS_DIR) $(DIST_DOCS_DIR)
+	$(MKDIR) -p $(DIST_SAMPLES_DIR)
+	$(CP) -r $(SAMPLES) $(DIST_SAMPLES_DIR)
+	@$(TOUCH) $@
+
+
+all: $(DIST_MARKER)
+
+TARGETS += all
+
+
+jmod: $(DIST_JMODS_MARKER)
+
+TARGETS += jmod
+
+
+native: $(NATIVE_MARKER)
+
+TARGETS += native
+
+
+.PHONY: $(TARGETS)

+
+# Copyright (c) 2018, 2025, Oracle and/or its affiliates. All rights reserved.
+# DO NOT ALTER OR REMOVE COPYRIGHT NOTICES OR THIS FILE HEADER.
+#
+# This code is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it
+# under the terms of the GNU General Public License version 2 only, as
+# published by the Free Software Foundation.  Oracle designates this
+# particular file as subject to the "Classpath" exception as provided
+# by Oracle in the LICENSE file that accompanied this code.
+#
+# This code is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, but WITHOUT
+# ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of MERCHANTABILITY or
+# FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU General Public License
+# version 2 for more details (a copy is included in the LICENSE file that
+# accompanied this code).
+#
+# You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License version
+# 2 along with this work; if not, write to the Free Software Foundation,
+# Inc., 51 Franklin St, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA 02110-1301 USA.
+#
+# Please contact Oracle, 500 Oracle Parkway, Redwood Shores, CA 94065 USA
+# or visit www.oracle.com if you need additional information or have any
+# questions.
+#
+
+include $(TOPDIR)/make/Common.gmk
+include $(TOPDIR)/make/SetupJava.gmk
+
+JVMV8_SRC_DIR := $(TOPDIR)/src
+
+JVMV8_JAVA_SRC_DIR := $(JVMV8_SRC_DIR)/java
+JVMV8_JAVA_SRCS := $(shell $(FIND) $(JVMV8_JAVA_SRC_DIR) -name "*.java")
+JVMV8_JAVA_RELEASE := 25
+
+JVMV8_JAVA_RESOURCES_DIR := $(JVMV8_JAVA_SRC_DIR)/org/openjdk/engine/javascript/internal/resources
+JVMV8_JAVA_RESOURCES := $(shell $(FIND) $(JVMV8_JAVA_RESOURCES_DIR) -type f)
+
+JVMV8_GEN_INCLUDE_DIR := $(JVMV8_BUILD_DIR)/include
+JVMV8_JAVA_CLASSES_DIR := $(JVMV8_BUILD_DIR)/classes
+JVMV8_JAVA_CLASSES_MARKER := $(MARKERS_DIR)/jvmv8.classes.marker
+# Provide a dummy directory for the redundant class files produced by javac -h
+JVMV8_DUMMY_JAVA_CLASSES_DIR := $(JVMV8_BUILD_DIR)/classes-dummy
+
+JVMV8_JAR_MANIFEST_FILE := $(JVMV8_JAVA_SRC_DIR)/META-INF/MANIFEST.MF
+JVMV8_JAR_MANIFEST_FILE_WITH_VERSION := $(JVMV8_BUILD_DIR)/MANIFEST.MF
+
+JVMV8_NATIVE_SRC_DIR := $(JVMV8_SRC_DIR)/native
+JVMV8_NATIVE_ALL_FILES := $(shell $(FIND) $(JVMV8_NATIVE_SRC_DIR) -type f)
+JVMV8_NATIVE_SRC_FILES := $(shell $(FIND) $(JVMV8_NATIVE_SRC_DIR) -name "*.cpp")
+
+JAVA_HOME_INCLUDES := $(addprefix -I,$(JAVA_HOME_INCLUDE_DIRS))
+
+JVMV8_DOCS_MARKER := $(MARKERS_DIR)/jvmv8.javadoc.marker
+
+CXXFLAGS := -shared -fPIC -fpermissive -std=c++20
+
+ifneq ($(DEBUG_LEVEL), debug)
+    CXXFLAGS += -g
+endif
+
+ifneq ($(HOST_CPU), $(TARGET_CPU))
+    CXXFLAGS += --target=$(CLANG_TARGET)
+endif
+
+CXXFLAGS += $(JAVA_HOME_INCLUDES)
+
+V8_CXXFLAGS := \
+    $(V8_DEFINES) \
+    -nostdinc++ \
+    $(addprefix -I,$(V8_INCLUDE_DIRS)) \
+    #
+
+CXXFLAGS += $(V8_CXXFLAGS)
+
+V8_MONOLITH_LIB := $(V8_BUILD_DIR)/obj/libv8_monolith.a
+
+V8_MONOLITH_LIB_NINJA := $(V8_BUILD_DIR)/obj/v8_monolith.ninja
+V8_MONOLITH_LIB_RLIB_DEPS_RAW := $(shell $(CAT) $(V8_MONOLITH_LIB_NINJA) | $(AWK) 'BEGIN { FOUND=0 }; /build obj\/libv8_monolith.a:/ { FOUND=1 }; FOUND && /  rlibs =/ { print; FOUND=0 }' | $(SED) 's/.*=[ ]*//')
+
+ifeq ($(V8_MONOLITH_LIB_RLIB_DEPS_RAW),)
+    # No rlibs involved -> no repackaging needed
+    JVMV8_V8_MONOLITH_RLIB :=
+else
+    V8_MONOLITH_LIB_RLIB_DEPS := $(addprefix $(V8_BUILD_DIR)/,$(V8_MONOLITH_LIB_RLIB_DEPS_RAW))
+
+    JVMV8_REPACK_V8_MONOLITH_DIR := $(JVMV8_BUILD_DIR)/v8_monolith_repack
+    JVMV8_V8_MONOLITH_RLIB := $(JVMV8_REPACK_V8_MONOLITH_DIR)/libv8_monolith_rlib.a
+endif
+
+
+ifeq ($(TARGET_OS), macos)
+    CXXFLAGS += \
+        -headerpad_max_install_names \
+        -isysroot /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/Platforms/MacOSX.platform/Developer/SDKs/MacOSX.sdk \
+        #
+
+    LLVM_AR := $(V8_REPO)/third_party/llvm-build/Release+Asserts/bin/llvm-ar
+    AR := $(LLVM_AR)
+
+    JVMV8_LINKFLAGS :=
+
+    # Set up link path (find the other dependency libraries next to the jvmv8 library)
+    JVMV8_LINKFLAGS += -Wl,-rpath,@loader_path/.
+
+    V8_LINKFLAGS :=
+
+    # libc++.a and libc++abi.a end up being "thin" archives on macOS and ld doesn't
+    # understand thin archives. Let's link with the corresponding/referenced object
+    # files explicitly instead
+    LIBCXX_A := $(V8_BUILD_DIR)/obj/buildtools/third_party/libc++/libc++.a
+    LIBCXX_OBJ_FILES := $(shell $(LLVM_AR) t $(LIBCXX_A))
+    LIBCXXABI_A := $(V8_BUILD_DIR)/obj/buildtools/third_party/libc++abi/libc++abi.a
+    LIBCXXABI_OBJ_FILES := $(shell $(LLVM_AR) t $(LIBCXXABI_A))
+
+    V8_LINKFLAGS += $(LIBCXX_OBJ_FILES) $(LIBCXXABI_OBJ_FILES)
+
+    V8_MONOLITH_LIB := $(V8_BUILD_DIR)/obj/libv8_monolith.a
+
+    V8_LINKFLAGS += $(V8_MONOLITH_LIB) $(JVMV8_V8_MONOLITH_RLIB)
+
+    JVMV8_LINKFLAGS += $(V8_LINKFLAGS)
+
+    JVMV8_LINKFLAGS += -framework CoreFoundation
+else ifeq ($(TARGET_OS), linux)
+    CXXFLAGS += \
+        -fuse-ld=lld \
+        --sysroot=$(V8_SYSROOT) \
+        #
+
+    JVMV8_LINKFLAGS := -pthread -ldl
+
+    # Set up link path: $ORIGIN and rpath (see: JDK-8326891)
+    JVMV8_LINKFLAGS += -Wl,-rpath,\$(DOLLAR)ORIGIN -Wl,--disable-new-dtags
+
+    # Set noexecstack
+    JVMV8_LINKFLAGS += -Wl,-z,noexecstack
+
+    # Error out on missing symbols
+    JVMV8_LINKFLAGS += -z defs
+
+    # Set up v8 linking
+    V8_LINKFLAGS :=
+
+    V8_LINKFLAGS += $(V8_BUILD_DIR)/obj/buildtools/third_party/libc++/libc++.a
+    V8_LINKFLAGS += $(V8_BUILD_DIR)/obj/buildtools/third_party/libc++abi/libc++abi.a
+
+    V8_LINKFLAGS += -Wl,--whole-archive $(V8_MONOLITH_LIB) -Wl,--no-whole-archive $(JVMV8_V8_MONOLITH_RLIB)
+
+    # This is more important than it might seem: without it, v8 crashes in
+    # mysterious ways, presumably because the wrong symbols/implementations
+    # are being picked up and used.
+    V8_LINKFLAGS += -Wl,--version-script=$(TOPDIR)/make/version-script.txt
+
+    V8_LINKFLAGS += $(V8_REPO)/third_party/llvm-build/Release+Asserts/lib/clang/22/lib/$(CLANG_TARGET)/libclang_rt.builtins.a
+
+    JVMV8_LINKFLAGS += $(V8_LINKFLAGS)
+else
+    $(error Unknown TARGET_OS: $(TARGET_OS))
+endif
+
+################################################################################
+
+
+ALL_TARGETS :=
+
+
+$(JVMV8_JAVA_CLASSES_MARKER): $(JVMV8_JAVA_SRCS)
+	$(MKDIR) -p $(@D)
+	$(MKDIR) -p $(JVMV8_JAVA_CLASSES_DIR)
+	$(JAVAC) \
+	  --release $(JVMV8_JAVA_RELEASE) \
+	  -Xlint:unchecked,deprecation \
+	  -sourcepath $(JVMV8_JAVA_SRC_DIR) \
+	  -d $(JVMV8_JAVA_CLASSES_DIR) \
+	  $(JVMV8_JAVA_SRCS)
+	$(TOUCH) $@
+
+
+$(JVMV8_DOCS_MARKER): $(JVMV8_JAVA_SRCS)
+	@$(MKDIR) -p $(@D)
+	@$(JAVADOC) \
+	  --release $(JVMV8_JAVA_RELEASE) \
+	  -sourcepath $(JVMV8_JAVA_SRC_DIR) \
+	  -d $(JVMV8_DOCS_DIR) \
+	  org.openjdk.engine.javascript
+	$(TOUCH) $@
+
+ALL_TARGETS += $(JVMV8_DOCS_MARKER)
+
+
+$(JVMV8_GEN_INCLUDE_DIR)/org_openjdk_engine_javascript_internal_V8.h: $(JVMV8_JAVA_CLASSES_MARKER) $(JVMV8_JAVA_SRC_DIR)/org/openjdk/engine/javascript/internal/V8.java
+	$(JAVAC) -cp $(JVMV8_JAVA_CLASSES_DIR) -h $(@D) -d $(JVMV8_DUMMY_JAVA_CLASSES_DIR) $(JVMV8_JAVA_SRC_DIR)/org/openjdk/engine/javascript/internal/V8.java
+
+
+$(JVMV8_JAR_MANIFEST_FILE_WITH_VERSION): $(JVMV8_JAR_MANIFEST_FILE)
+	$(MKDIR) -p $(@D)
+	$(CP) $(JVMV8_JAR_MANIFEST_FILE) $@.tmp
+	$(ECHO) "Implementation-Version: $(DETROIT_BUILD_VER)" >> $@.tmp
+	$(MV) $@.tmp $@
+
+
+$(JVMV8_JAR): $(JVMV8_JAR_MANIFEST_FILE_WITH_VERSION) $(JVMV8_JAVA_CLASSES_MARKER) $(JVMV8_JAVA_RESOURCES) $(JVMV8_LIB)
+	$(MKDIR) -p $(@D)
+	$(CP) -r $(JVMV8_JAVA_RESOURCES_DIR) $(JVMV8_JAVA_CLASSES_DIR)/org/openjdk/engine/javascript/internal
+	$(CP) -r $(JVMV8_LIB) $(JVMV8_JAVA_CLASSES_DIR)/org/openjdk/engine/javascript/internal
+	$(CP) -r $(JVMV8_JAVA_SRC_DIR)/META-INF $(JVMV8_JAVA_CLASSES_DIR)
+	$(JAR) cfm $(JVMV8_JAR) $(JVMV8_JAR_MANIFEST_FILE_WITH_VERSION) -C $(JVMV8_JAVA_CLASSES_DIR) .
+
+ALL_TARGETS += $(JVMV8_JAR)
+
+ifneq ($(JVMV8_V8_MONOLITH_RLIB), )
+$(JVMV8_V8_MONOLITH_RLIB): $(V8_MONOLITH_LIB_RLIB_DEPS)
+	@$(MKDIR) -p $(@D)
+	$(RM) -rf $(JVMV8_REPACK_V8_MONOLITH_DIR)/obj
+	@$(MKDIR) -p $(JVMV8_REPACK_V8_MONOLITH_DIR)/obj
+	($(CD) $(JVMV8_REPACK_V8_MONOLITH_DIR)/obj && \
+	    for rlib in $(V8_MONOLITH_LIB_RLIB_DEPS); do \
+	        $(MKDIR) -p $$(basename $$rlib); \
+		($(CD) $$(basename $$rlib) && $(AR) x $$rlib); \
+	    done \
+	)
+	($(CD) $(JVMV8_REPACK_V8_MONOLITH_DIR)/obj && $(AR) -r -c -D $@.tmp $$($(FIND) . -name "*.o"))
+	$(MV) $@.tmp $@
+endif
+
+$(JVMV8_LIB): $(JVMV8_GEN_INCLUDE_DIR)/org_openjdk_engine_javascript_internal_V8.h $(JVMV8_NATIVE_ALL_FILES) $(V8_MONOLITH_LIB) $(JVMV8_V8_MONOLITH_RLIB)
+	$(MKDIR) -p $(@D)
+	$(V8_CXX) -I$(JVMV8_GEN_INCLUDE_DIR) $(CXXFLAGS) $(JVMV8_NATIVE_SRC_FILES) $(JVMV8_LINKFLAGS) -o $@.tmp
+ifeq ($(TARGET_OS), macos)
+	$(INSTALL_NAME_TOOL) -id @rpath/$(JVMV8_LIB_FILE_NAME) $@.tmp
+endif
+	$(MV) $@.tmp $@
+
+ALL_TARGETS += $(JVMV8_LIB)
+
+
+
+####
+#
+# Exported targets
+#
+
+# Build the jvmv8 jar and native library
+all: $(ALL_TARGETS)
+
+TARGETS += all
+
+
+# Build the jvmv8 jar file
+jar: $(JVMV8_JAR)
+
+TARGETS += jar
+
+
+# Build the jvmv8 native library
+jvmv8: $(JVMV8_LIB)
+
+TARGETS += jvmv8
+
+
+# Regenerate the jvmv8_java_classes.hpp jvmv8_java_classes.cpp files
+genjavaclasses:
+	jjs -scripting $(TOPDIR)/genjavaclasses -- $(JVMV8_NATIVE_SRC_DIR)
+
+TARGETS += genjavaclasses
+
+
+javadoc: $(JVMV8_DOCS_MARKER)
+
+TARGETS += javadoc
+
+.PHONY: $(TARGETS)
+

+include $(TOPDIR)/make/Common.gmk
+
+MAKE_ARGS := TOPDIR=$(TOPDIR)
+
+all: dist
+
+TARGETS += all
+
+
+jvmv8: v8
+	$(CD) $(TOPDIR)/make && $(MAKE) $(MAKE_ARGS) -f Jvmv8.gmk all
+
+TARGETS += jvmv8
+
+
+get-v8:
+	$(CD) $(TOPDIR)/make && $(MAKE) $(MAKE_ARGS) -f V8.gmk $@
+
+TARGETS += v8 get-v8
+
+
+v8:
+	$(CD) $(TOPDIR)/make && $(MAKE) $(MAKE_ARGS) -f V8.gmk all
+
+TARGETS += v8 get-v8
+
+
+dist: jvmv8
+	$(CD) $(TOPDIR)/make && $(MAKE) $(MAKE_ARGS) -f Dist.gmk all
+
+TARGETS += dist
+
+
+bundles: dist
+	$(CD) $(TOPDIR)/make && $(MAKE) $(MAKE_ARGS) -f Bundles.gmk all
+
+TARGETS += bundles
+
+
+TEST_TARGETS_INTERACTIVE := runshell
+TEST_TARGETS := test quicktest helloworld gcstresscontexts gcstressisolates
+$(TEST_TARGETS) $(TEST_TARGETS_INTERACTIVE): dist
+	$(CD) $(TOPDIR)/make && $(MAKE) $(MAKE_ARGS) -f Test.gmk $@
+
+TARGETS += $(TEST_TARGETS)
+
+
+test-only:
+	$(CD) $(TOPDIR)/make && $(MAKE) $(MAKE_ARGS) -f Test.gmk $@
+
+TARGETS += test-only
+
+
+test-all: $(TEST_TARGETS)
+
+TARGETS += test-all
+
+
+clean:
+	rm -fr $(BUILD_DIR)
+
+TARGETS += clean
+
+
+####
+#
+# Since v8 takes so (relatively) long to build, and we so rarely change it
+# this convenience target makes it possible to do clean everything in the
+# build dir _except_ v8.
+#
+V8_DIR := $(BUILD_DIR)/$(firstword $(subst /,$(SPACE),$(patsubst $(BUILD_DIR)/%,%,$(V8_BUILD_DIR))))
+ALL_BUT_V8 := $(filter-out $(V8_DIR),$(wildcard $(BUILD_DIR)/*))
+
+clean-all-but-v8:
+	$(RM) -fr $(ALL_BUT_V8)
+
+TARGETS += clean-all-but-v8
+
+.PHONY: $(TARGETS)

+
+include $(TOPDIR)/make/Common.gmk
+include $(TOPDIR)/make/SetupJava.gmk
+
+ifneq ($(HOST_PLATFORM), $(TARGET_PLATFORM))
+    $(info WARNING: Trying to run tests on a cross build:)
+    $(info    Host:   $(HOST_PLATFORM))
+    $(info    Target: $(TARGET_PLATFORM))
+    $(info This is unlikely to work, but continuing nevertheless...)
+endif
+
+ifeq ($(TEST),)
+    TEST := .
+endif
+
+TEST_DIR := $(TOPDIR)/test
+
+ifneq ($(JT_HOME),)
+    JTREG := $(JT_HOME)/bin/jtreg
+else
+    JTREG := jtreg
+endif
+
+TEST_SUPPORT_DIR := $(BUILD_DIR)/test-support
+JTREG_WORK_DIR := $(TEST_SUPPORT_DIR)/JTwork
+JTREG_REPORT_DIR := $(TEST_SUPPORT_DIR)/JTreport
+
+JTREG_BASE_ARGS := \
+    -workDir:$(JTREG_WORK_DIR) \
+    -reportDir:$(JTREG_REPORT_DIR) \
+    -cpa:$(JVMV8_JAR) \
+    -conc:auto \
+    -verbose:summary,fail,error \
+    -retain:fail,error \
+    #
+
+DIST_JAVA_OPTIONS_AS_VMOPTIONS := $(addprefix -vmoption:,$(DIST_JAVA_OPTIONS))
+
+JTREG_BASE_ARGS += \
+    -jdk:$(JAVA_HOME) \
+    $(DIST_JAVA_OPTIONS_AS_VMOPTIONS) \
+    #
+
+SHELL_CLASS_NAME := org.openjdk.engine.javascript.Main
+
+
+# Run all the jtreg tests
+test test-only:
+	@$(JTREG) -version > /dev/null 2>&1 || ($(ECHO) "Failed to execute $(JTREG), please set JT_HOME or add jtreg to your PATH"; exit 1)
+	@$(RM) -rf $(JTREG_WORK_DIR) $(JTREG_REPORT_DIR)
+	@$(MKDIR) -p $(JTREG_WORK_DIR) $(JTREG_REPORT_DIR)
+	$(CD) $(TEST_DIR) && $(JTREG) $(JTREG_BASE_ARGS) $(TEST)
+
+
+# Run jvmv8 as a shell
+runshell:
+	@$(JAVA) $(DIST_JAVA_OPTIONS) $(SHELL_CLASS_NAME) -cp . -l v8
+
+
+# Run a quick smoke test
+quicktest:
+	@$(MKDIR) -p $(BUILD_DIR)/tmp
+	@$(CAT) $(TEST_DIR)/assert.js $(TEST_DIR)/test.js > $(BUILD_DIR)/tmp/test.js
+	$(JAVA) $(DIST_JAVA_OPTIONS) $(SHELL_CLASS_NAME) -cp . -l v8 $(BUILD_DIR)/tmp/test.js
+
+
+# Run a quick smoke test
+helloworld:
+	@$(MKDIR) -p $(BUILD_DIR)/tmp
+	@$(CAT) $(TEST_DIR)/assert.js $(TEST_DIR)/helloworld.js > $(BUILD_DIR)/tmp/helloworld.js
+	@$(JAVA) $(DIST_JAVA_OPTIONS) $(SHELL_CLASS_NAME) -cp . -l v8 $(BUILD_DIR)/tmp/helloworld.js
+
+
+# Run the context stress test
+gcstresscontexts:
+	@$(JAVAC) $(TEST_DIR)/GCStressContexts.java -d $(BUILD_DIR)
+	@$(JAVA) $(DIST_JAVA_OPTIONS) $(RUN_FLAGS) -cp $(BUILD_DIR) GCStressContexts 1000 false >$(BUILD_DIR)/gcstresscontexts 2>&1
+
+
+# Run the isolate stress test
+gcstressisolates:
+	@$(JAVAC) $(TEST_DIR)/GCStressIsolates.java -d $(BUILD_DIR)
+	@$(JAVA) $(DIST_JAVA_OPTIONS) $(RUN_FLAGS) -cp $(BUILD_DIR) GCStressIsolates 1000 false >$(BUILD_DIR)/gcstressisolates 2>&1

+# Inc., 51 Franklin St, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA 02110-1301 USA.
+#

+# or visit www.oracle.com if you need additional information or have any
+# questions.
+#
+
+include $(TOPDIR)/make/Common.gmk
+
+####
+#
+# Depot tools
+#
+
+ifeq ($(DEPOT_TOOLS),)
+    GCLIENT_MAYBE := $(shell command -v gclient)
+    ifeq ($(GCLIENT_MAYBE),)
+        $(error Could not locate depot tools using PATH, please set DEPOT_TOOLS or add to PATH)
+    endif
+    DEPOT_TOOLS := $(dir $(GCLIENT_MAYBE))
+endif
+
+FETCH := $(DEPOT_TOOLS)fetch
+GCLIENT := $(DEPOT_TOOLS)gclient
+GN := $(DEPOT_TOOLS)gn
+
+
+ifeq ($(V8_REVISION),)
+    $(error V8_REVISION not set)
+endif
+
+V8_CONF_FILE := $(V8_CONF_DIR)/$(V8_TARGET_CPU).args.gn
+
+V8_BUILD_MARKER := $(MARKERS_DIR)/v8.build.marker
+
+all: build
+
+TARGETS += all
+
+
+get-v8: $(V8_REPO)
+
+TARGETS += get-v8
+
+
+build: $(V8_BUILD_MARKER)
+
+TARGETS += build
+
+
+$(V8_BUILD_MARKER): .FORCE $(V8_CONF_FILE)
+	$(MKDIR) -p $(@D)
+	$(MKDIR) -p $(V8_BUILD_DIR)
+	$(CP) $(V8_CONF_FILE) $(V8_BUILD_DIR)/args.gn
+	$(CD) $(V8_REPO) && \
+	$(GN) gen $(V8_BUILD_DIR) && \
+	$(NINJA) -C $(V8_BUILD_DIR)
+	$(TOUCH) $@
+
+
+$(V8_REPO):
+	@$(MKDIR) -p $(@D)
+	@$(CD) $(@D) && $(FETCH) v8
+	@$(CD) $(V8_REPO) && $(GCLIENT) sync -r $(V8_REVISION)
+ifeq ($(HOST_OS), linux)
+# Always install the arm64 sysroot to ensure it's theere for cross compilation
+	@$(CD) $(V8_REPO) && build/linux/sysroot_scripts/install-sysroot.py --arch=arm64
+endif
+
+
+switchv8-stable:
+	@$(CD) $(V8_REPO) && \
+	$(GIT) fetch && \
+	$(GIT) switch chromium/5481
+	$(DEPOT_TOOLS_GCLIENT) sync -D
+	$(DEPOT_TOOLS_GCLIENT) sync --with_branch_heads --with_tags
+
+TARGETS += switchv8-stable
+
+
+switchv8-tip:
+	@$(CD) $(V8_REPO) && \
+	$(GIT) fetch && \
+	$(GIT) switch master
+	$(DEPOT_TOOLS_GCLIENT) sync -D
+	$(DEPOT_TOOLS_GCLIENT) sync --with_branch_heads --with_tags
+
+TARGETS += switchv8-tip
+
+
+.PHONY: $(TARGETS)
+.FORCE:

+#!/bin/bash
+#
+# Copyright (c) 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates. All rights reserved.
+#
+# Redistribution and use in source and binary forms, with or without
+# modification, are permitted provided that the following conditions

+# IS" AND ANY EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTIES, INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO,
+# THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR
+# PURPOSE ARE DISCLAIMED.  IN NO EVENT SHALL THE COPYRIGHT OWNER OR
+# CONTRIBUTORS BE LIABLE FOR ANY DIRECT, INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL,
+# EXEMPLARY, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES (INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO,
+# PROCUREMENT OF SUBSTITUTE GOODS OR SERVICES; LOSS OF USE, DATA, OR
+# PROFITS; OR BUSINESS INTERRUPTION) HOWEVER CAUSED AND ON ANY THEORY OF
+# LIABILITY, WHETHER IN CONTRACT, STRICT LIABILITY, OR TORT (INCLUDING
+# NEGLIGENCE OR OTHERWISE) ARISING IN ANY WAY OUT OF THE USE OF THIS
+# SOFTWARE, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGE.
+
+SCRIPTDIR=$(dirname $0)
+
+if [ -z "$1" ]; then
+    echo "USAGE: bash ./run-java-sample.sh Sample.java"
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+sample_file=$1
+args_array=( "$@" )
+script_args=${args_array[@]:1}
+
+if [ ! -f $sample_file ]; then
+    echo "The specified file '$sample_file' does not exist"
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+builds=($(ls ${SCRIPTDIR}/build))
+
+if [ ${#builds[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
+  echo "No build found. Run 'make dist' first"
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+if [ ${#builds[@]} -gt 1 ]; then
+  echo "Multiple builds found, picking '${builds[0]}'"
+fi
+
+build=${builds[0]} # just pick the first one
+lib=${SCRIPTDIR}/build/$build/dist/org.openjdk.engine.javascript/lib
+
+if [ ! -f $lib/org.openjdk.engine.javascript.jar ]; then
+    echo "org.openjdk.engine.javascript.jar not found in '$lib'"
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+java \
+  --module-path $lib \
+  --add-modules org.openjdk.engine.javascript \
+  --enable-native-access=org.openjdk.engine.javascript \
+  $sample_file $script_args

+// example demonstrating that arbitrary Lambda can be called as function from script
+
+public class LambdaAsFunc {
+    public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception {
+        ScriptEngineManager m = new ScriptEngineManager();
+        ScriptEngine e = m.getEngineByName("v8");
+
+        // expose a lambda as top-level script function
+        e.put("upper", (Function<String, String>) String::toUpperCase);
+
+        // call lambda as though it is a normal script function
+        System.out.println(e.eval("upper('hello')"));
+    }
+}

+// Usage: ../jjs.sh array_mapreduce.js
+
+// Many Array.prototype functions such as map,
+// filter, reduce, reduceRight, every, some are generic.
+// These functions accept ECMAScript array as well as
+// many array-like objects including java arrays.
+// So, you can do map/filter/reduce with Java streams or
+// you can also use Array.prototype functions as below.
+
+var DoubleArray = Java.type("double[]");
+var StringArray = Java.type("java.lang.String[]");
+
+var map = Array.prototype.map;
+var filter = Array.prototype.filter;
+var reduce = Array.prototype.reduce;
+
+var jarr = new StringArray(5);
+jarr[0] = "jvmv8";
+jarr[1] = "ecmascript";
+jarr[2] = "javascript";
+jarr[3] = "js";
+jarr[4] = "scheme";
+
+// sum of word lengths
+print("Sum word length:",
+    reduce.call(
+        map.call(jarr, x => x.length),
+        (x, y) => x + y)
+);
+
+// another array example involving numbers
+jarr = new DoubleArray(10);
+// make random array of numbers
+for (var i = 0; i < jarr.length; i++)
+    jarr[i] = Math.random();
+
+var forEach = Array.prototype.forEach;
+// print numbers in the array
+forEach.call(jarr, x => print(x));
+
+// print sum of squares of the random numbers
+print("Square sum:",
+    reduce.call(
+        map.call(jarr, x => x*x),
+        (x, y) => x + y)
+);

+
+
+// Find the file with maximum number of lines
+
+var Files = Java.type("java.nio.file.Files");
+var Integer = Java.type("java.lang.Integer");
+var Paths = Java.type("java.nio.file.Paths");
+
+var file = arguments.length == 0? "." : arguments[0];
+var ext = arguments.length > 1? arguments[1] : ".java";
+var path = Paths.get(file);
+
+// return number of lines in given Path (that represents a file)
+function lines(p) {
+    var strm = Files.lines(p);
+    try {
+        return strm.count();
+    } finally {
+        strm.close();
+    }
+}
+
+// walk files, map to file and lines, find the max
+var obj = Files.find(path, Integer.MAX_VALUE,
+    (p, a) => !p.toFile().isDirectory() && p.toString().endsWith(ext)).
+map(p => ({ path : p, lines: lines(p) })).
+max((x, y) => x.lines - y.lines).
+get();
+
+// print path and lines of the max line file
+print(obj.path, obj.lines);

+
+// Simple sample demonstrating "fixed point" computation with Streams
+
+var Stream = Java.type("java.util.stream.Stream");
+
+// generic fixed point procedure
+function fixed_point(f, init_guess) {
+  const tolerance = 0.00001;
+  const close_enough = (v1, v2) => Math.abs(v1 - v2) < tolerance;
+
+  var prev;
+  return Stream.iterate(init_guess, f)
+      .filter(x => {
+          try {
+              return prev == undefined? false : close_enough(prev, x);
+          } finally {
+              prev = x;
+          }
+      })
+      .findFirst()
+      .get();
+}
+
+// solution to x = cos(x)
+print(fixed_point(Math.cos, 1.0))
+
+// solution to x = sin(x) + cos(x)
+print(fixed_point(x => Math.sin(x) + Math.cos(x), 1.0));
+
+// square root by Newton's method
+const sqrt = n => fixed_point(x => (x + n/x) / 2, 2.0);
+
+print(sqrt(2))
+print(sqrt(3))
+
+

+ * PROFITS; OR BUSINESS INTERRUPTION) HOWEVER CAUSED AND ON ANY THEORY OF
+ * LIABILITY, WHETHER IN CONTRACT, STRICT LIABILITY, OR TORT (INCLUDING
+ * NEGLIGENCE OR OTHERWISE) ARISING IN ANY WAY OUT OF THE USE OF THIS
+ * SOFTWARE, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGE.
+ */
+
+
+// bind on a Java constructor
+var bind = Function.prototype.bind;
+
+var URL = Java.type("java.net.URL");
+
+// get the constructor that accepts URL, String parameters.
+// constructor signatures are properties of type object.
+var newURL = URL["(java.net.URL, java.lang.String)"];
+
+// bind "context" URL parameter.
+var DevJavaURL = bind.call(newURL, null, new URL('https://dev.java/'));
+
+// now you can create context relative URLs using the bound constructor
+print(new DevJavaURL("learn"));
+
+// read the URL content and print (optional part)
+
+var BufferedReader = Java.type("java.io.BufferedReader");
+var InputStreamReader = Java.type("java.io.InputStreamReader");
+
+// function to retrieve text content of the given URL
+function readTextFromURL(url) {
+    var str = '';
+    var reader = new BufferedReader(
+        new InputStreamReader(url.openStream()));
+    try {
+        reader.lines().forEach(x => str += x);
+        return str;
+    } finally {
+        reader.close();
+    }
+}
+
+print(readTextFromURL(new DevJavaURL("learn")));

+ * PROFITS; OR BUSINESS INTERRUPTION) HOWEVER CAUSED AND ON ANY THEORY OF
+ * LIABILITY, WHETHER IN CONTRACT, STRICT LIABILITY, OR TORT (INCLUDING
+ * NEGLIGENCE OR OTHERWISE) ARISING IN ANY WAY OUT OF THE USE OF THIS
+ * SOFTWARE, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGE.
+ */
+
+
+// JavaImporter along with 'with' statement helps in
+// localized Java class references
+
+function readTextFromURL(url) {
+
+    // equivalent to
+    //
+    //    import java.io.*;
+    //    import java.net.*;
+    //    import java.lang.StringBuffer;
+    //
+    // only inside the 'with' statement
+    with (new JavaImporter(java.io,
+        java.net,
+        java.lang.StringBuilder)) {
+        var buf = new StringBuilder();
+        var u = new URL(url);
+        var reader = new BufferedReader(
+            new InputStreamReader(u.openStream()));
+        var line = null;
+        try {
+            while ((line = reader.readLine()) != null)
+                buf.append(line).append('\n');
+        } finally {
+            reader.close();
+        }
+
+        return buf.toString();
+    }
+}
+
+print(readTextFromURL("https://dev.java/"));

+// Simple program that lists available locals. This is ECMAScript
+// port of Java example by @brunoborges
+
+// for String.prototype.compareTo
+load("jvmv8:nashorn_compat.js");
+
+// Java classes used
+var Arrays = Java.type("java.util.Arrays");
+var Collectors = Java.type("java.util.stream.Collectors");
+var JString = Java.type("java.lang.String");
+var Locale = Java.type("java.util.Locale");
+
+var formatStr = "Country : %s \t\t\t\t:\t Country Code : %s";
+
+// jvmv8 allows script functions to be passed
+// whereever Java8 lambdas are expected.
+
+// The following prints locales in (country) display name order
+var list = Arrays.asList(Locale.getISOCountries())
+    .stream()
+    .map(x => new Locale("", x))
+    .sorted((c0, c1) => c0.displayCountry.compareTo(c1.displayCountry))
+    .map(l => JString.format(formatStr, l.displayCountry, l.country))
+    .collect(Collectors.toList());
+
+list.forEach(print);

+
+var map = new java.util.HashMap();
+map.put("foo", 34);
+map.put("bar", "hello");
+
+var obj = new Proxy({}, {
+    get: function(target, name) {
+        return map.get(name);
+    },
+
+    has: function(target, name) {
+        return map.containsKey(name);
+    }
+});
+
+with(obj) {
+   print(foo);
+   print(bar);
+}

+// print "Mother's day" of the given year using Java Time API
+
+if (arguments.length == 0) {
+    print("Usage: ../jjs.sh mothers_day.js year");
+    java.lang.System.exit(1);
+}
+
+// java classes used
+var DayOfWeek = java.time.DayOfWeek;
+var LocalDate = java.time.LocalDate;
+var TemporalAdjusters = java.time.temporal.TemporalAdjusters;
+
+var year = parseInt(arguments[0]);
+
+// We need second Sunday of May. Make April 30 of the given
+// year and adjust to next Sunday from there twice. To adjust a Date
+// we use a common TemporalAdjuster provided in JDK8.
+// https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/api/java/time/temporal/TemporalAdjusters.html
+
+print(LocalDate.of(year, 4, 30).
+    with(TemporalAdjusters.next(DayOfWeek.SUNDAY)).
+    with(TemporalAdjusters.next(DayOfWeek.SUNDAY)));

+// Simple password generator using SecureRandom + stream API
+
+// accept optional password length from command line
+var len = arguments.length? parseInt(arguments[0]) : 8;
+
+// Java types used
+var Collectors = Java.type("java.util.stream.Collectors");
+var SecureRandom = Java.type("java.security.SecureRandom");
+
+// allowed password chars
+var chars =
+ "!@#$%ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789";
+
+// generated and print password
+print(new SecureRandom().
+    ints(len, 0, chars.length).
+    mapToObj(i => chars[i]).
+    collect(Collectors.joining("")));

+ */
+
+
+// Print all symbolic links in user's home directory
+
+// JavaImporter and "with" to simulate "import" statements in Java.
+
+with (new JavaImporter(java.nio.file, java.lang)) {
+
+  var userDir = System.getProperty("user.dir")
+  var home = FileSystems.default.getPath(userDir);
+
+  // JS arrows can be passed where Java lambdas are required.
+  Files.walk(home).
+    filter(p => Files.isSymbolicLink(p)).
+    forEach(p => print(p, '->', Files.readSymbolicLink(p)));
+}

+
+// v8 supports 'stack' property on ECMAScript
+// error objects. This property's value is a string
+// that shows script stack trace.
+
+function g() {
+    throw new Error("wrong");
+}
+
+function f() {
+    g();
+}
+
+// Output looks something like:
+//
+// Error: wrong
+//    at g (stack.js:37:11)
+//    at f (stack.js:41:5)
+//    at stack.js:52:4
+
+try {
+   f();
+} catch (e) {
+   print(e.stack);
+}

+

+
+// pass script function when a lambda is required
+// Math.random passed here for double generator lambda
+// print passed to forEach method
+
+DoubleStream
+    .generate(Math.random)
+    .limit(100)
+    .forEach(print);
+

+print(DoubleStream
+    .generate(Math.random)
+    .limit(100)
+    .summaryStatistics());

+// Usage: ../jjs.sh uniq.js
+// or: ../jvmv8 uniq.js <file>
+
+// omit repeated lines and print unique lines
+
+var BufferedReader = Java.type("java.io.BufferedReader");
+var FileReader = Java.type("java.io.FileReader");
+var InputStreamReader = Java.type("java.io.InputStreamReader");
+var System = Java.type("java.lang.System");
+
+// use object as set - but insertion order preserved
+var uniqueLines = {};
+var reader = arguments.length > 0 ?
+    new FileReader(arguments[0])  :
+    new InputStreamReader(System.in);
+reader = new BufferedReader(reader);
+
+// add unique lines
+reader.lines().forEach(line => {
+    uniqueLines[line] = true;
+})
+
+for (line in uniqueLines) {
+    print(line);
+}

+ * This example demonstrates how to print word histogram
+ * of a given text file using regex, array and JSON
+ * functions.
+ */
+
+if (arguments.length < 1) {
+    print("Usage: ../jjs.sh word_histogram.js <file>");
+    java.lang.System.exit(1);
+}
+
+load("jvmv8:nashorn_compat.js");
+
+var obj = {};
+
+readFully(arguments[0]).
+    split(/[^\w+]/).
+    forEach(x => (x in obj? obj[x]++ : obj[x] = 1));
+
+print(JSON.stringify(obj));
+

+

+fs.close()

+package org.openjdk.engine.javascript;
+
+/**
+ * JSPromise is the public interface to V8 Promise objects.
+ */
+public interface JSPromise extends JSObject {
+    /**
+     * The _catch method returns a Promise and deals with rejected cases only.
+     * This is equivalent to JavaScript Promise.prototype.catch method.
+     *
+     * @param handler function called when the promise fails.
+     * @return the promise that deals with rejected cases only.
+     */
+    public JSPromise _catch(JSFunction handler);
+
+    /**
+     * The then method returns a Promise and deals with fulfilled cases only.
+     *
+     * @param handler function called when the promise is resolved.
+     * @return the promise that deals with fulfilled cases only.
+     */
+    public JSPromise then(JSFunction handler);
+
+    /**
+     * Returns true if the promise has at least one derived promise, and therefore
+     * resolve/reject handlers (including default handler).
+     *
+     * @return true if the promise has at least one derived promise or not.
+     */
+    public boolean hasHandler();
+}

+package org.openjdk.engine.javascript;
+
+/**
+ * Interface to control V8 script engine's execution. For example, long running
+ * "runaway" scripts can be interrupted and/or terminated using this interface.
+ */
+public interface V8ExecutionControl {
+    /**
+     * Forcefully terminate the current thread of JavaScript execution in the given engine.
+     */
+    public void terminateExecution();
+
+    /**
+     * Request V8 to interrupt long running JavaScript code and invoke the given callback.
+     * After callback returns, the control will be returned to the JavaScript code.
+     * Registered callback must not reenter interrupted engine for JavaScript evaluation.
+     *
+     * @param callback callback that is invoked on interruption.
+     * @throws NullPointerException if callback is null.
+     */
+    public void requestInterrupt(Runnable callback);
+
+    /**
+     * Runs the Microtask Work Queue until empty Any exceptions thrown by microtask
+     * callbacks are swallowed.
+     */
+    public void runMicrotasks();
+
+    /**
+     * Enqueues the callback to the Microtask Work Queue.
+     *
+     * @param microtask callback function for the microtask.
+     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if microtask is not function from this engine.
+     * @throws NullPointerException if microtask is null.
+     */
+    public void enqueueMicrotask(JSFunction microtask);
+
+    /**
+     * Schedules a JavaScript exception to be thrown when returning to JavaScript. When an exception
+     * has been scheduled it is illegal to invoke any JavaScript operation; the caller must
+     * return immediately. The Void return type is used so that the caller can immediately return.
+     *
+     * @param exception V8 exception to be thrown.
+     * @return null always. Return type provided so that caller immediately return.
+     */
+    public Object throwException(Object exception);
+
+    /**
+     * Returns array javascript stack frames from the current javascript stack.
+     *
+     * @return array of javascript stack frames, null if not available.
+     */
+    public StackTraceElement[] getCurrentStackTrace();
+}

+package org.openjdk.engine.javascript;
+
+/**
+ * Interface to V8 Inspector.
+ */
+public interface V8Inspector {
+    /**
+     * Listener interface to receive messages/calls from V8 inspector.
+     */
+    public interface Listener {
+        /**
+         * Called to send a response message from V8 inspector to the front-end.
+         *
+         * @param msg the JSON String messsage.
+         */
+        public void onResponse(String msg);
+
+        /**
+         * Called to send a notification message from V8 inspector to the front-end.
+         *
+         * @param msg the JSON String messsage.
+         */
+        public void onNotification(String msg);
+
+        /**
+         * Run nested message loop when V8 is paused on a breakpoint.
+         */
+        public void runMessageLoopOnPause();
+
+        /**
+         * Quit nested message loop entered when V8 is paused on a breakpoint.
+         */
+        public void quitMessageLoopOnPause();
+    }
+
+    /**
+     * Set the V8 Inspector listener.
+     *
+     * @param listener listener to process messages from V8 inspector.
+     */
+    public void setListener(Listener listener);
+
+    /**
+     * Pass JSON protocol message from the front-end to the V8 inspector.
+     *
+     * @param msg JSON protocol message from the front-end.
+     */
+    public void dispatchProtocolMessage(String msg);
+}

+package org.openjdk.engine.javascript;
+
+import javax.script.ScriptContext;
+import javax.script.ScriptException;
+
+/**
+ * V8 engine specific script compilation interface.
+ */
+public interface V8ScriptCompiler {
+    /**
+     * Compile a script function. This is equivalent to compiling the following:
+     *
+     *     with (obj) { return function(args) { ... } }
+     *
+     * It is possible to specify multiple context extensions (obj in the above example).
+     *
+     * @param script script to be compiled
+     * @param arguments names of arguments to the function (can be null)
+     * @param extensions zero or more "scope" objects serving as "extensions" (can be null)
+     * @param context ScriptContext to use for script compilation
+     * @return compiled script function
+     *
+     * @throws NullPointerException if script is null
+     * @throws NullPointerException if context is null
+     * @throws ScriptException if script compilation fails
+     */
+    public JSObject compileFunction(String script, String[] arguments,
+                JSObject[] extensions, ScriptContext context) throws ScriptException;
+
+    /**
+     * Compile a script function. This is equivalent to compiling the following:
+     *
+     *     with (obj) { return function(args) { ... } }
+     *
+     * It is possible to specify multiple context extensions (obj in the above example).
+     *
+     * @param script script to be compiled
+     * @param arguments names of arguments to the function (can be null)
+     * @param extensions zero or more "scope" objects serving as "extensions" (can be null)
+     * @return compiled script function
+     *
+     * @throws NullPointerException if script is null
+     * @throws ScriptException if script compilation fails
+     */
+    public JSObject compileFunction(String script, String[] arguments,
+                JSObject[] extensions) throws ScriptException;
+}
+

+import javax.script.Invocable;
+import javax.script.ScriptContext;
+import javax.script.ScriptEngine;
+import javax.script.ScriptException;
+
+/**
+ * This interface contains additional API points unique to the V8ScriptEngine implementation
+ */
+public sealed interface V8ScriptEngine extends ScriptEngine, Compilable, Invocable,
+        AutoCloseable, V8ExecutionControl, V8ScriptCompiler permits V8ScriptEngineImpl {
+
+    /** The ScriptContext attribute key for the module resolver */
+    String MODULE_RESOLVER = "org.openjdk.engine.javascript.moduleResolver";
+
+    /**
+     * Load a javascript module from source
+     *
+     * @param moduleSource the source of the module
+     * @return the namespace object of the module
+     */
+    JSObject loadModule(String moduleSource) throws ScriptException;
+
+    /**
+     * Load a javascript module from source
+     *
+     * @param moduleSource the source of the module
+     * @param sc the script context to use when loading the module
+     * @return the namespace object of the module
+     */
+    JSObject loadModule(String moduleSource, ScriptContext sc) throws ScriptException;
+
+    /**
+     * Get the V8Inspector object to interact with the inspector.
+     *
+     * @return V8Inspector object.
+     * @throws IllegalStateException if the engine is not initialized with V8 inspector enabled
+     */
+    V8Inspector getInspector();
+}

+package org.openjdk.engine.javascript.internal;
+
+/**
+ * This is an option class that at its most primitive level just wraps a
+ * boolean or String. However, it is conceivable that the option, when set
+ * should run some initializations (for example, the logger system) or carry
+ * some other kind of payload, arrays, Collections, etc. In that case, this
+ * should be subclassed
+ *
+ * @param <T> option value type, for example a boolean or something more complex
+ */
+class Option<T> {
+    /** the option value */
+    protected T value;
+
+    Option(final T value) {
+       this.value = value;
+    }
+
+    /**
+     * Return the value of an option
+     * @return the option value
+     */
+    public T getValue() {
+        return value;
+    }
+
+    @Override
+    public String toString() {
+        return getValue() + " [" + getValue().getClass() + "]";
+    }
+}

+# questions.
+#
+
+option.error.invalid.option="{0}" is not a recognized option. Use "{1}" or "{2}" to see a list of all supported options.
+
+jvmv8.options = Usage: jvmv8 [option=value]* [filename.js]+ [-- options...]\n\
+                \n\
+                Valid options are:
+
+#
+# Localized meta descriptions
+#
+jvmv8.options.param = param
+jvmv8.options.default = default
+
+#
+# Which option is "help".
+#
+jvmv8.options.help.key = jvmv8.option.help
+
+#
+# Which option is "xhelp".
+#
+jvmv8.options.xhelp.key = jvmv8.option.xhelp
+
+#
+# Which option is "D".
+#
+jvmv8.options.D.key = jvmv8.option.D
+
+##
+## Grammar: at least short or long form. Arguments are optional, in that case they are given as
+##
+## params - a description of the parameters for command line usage
+## name - the name of the option, e.g. "--long-name"
+## short_name - the short name of the option, e.g. "-l"
+## type - the type of the option, currently allowed: boolean, integer, string, log, timezone. defaults to boolean if left out
+## is_undocumented - should this option never appear in the online help. defaults to no.
+## desc - description of what the option does
+## default - default value of the option. e.g. debug.lines is true by default. Not set means option not available by default
+## dependency - does this arg imply another arg.
+## confict - does this arg conflict with another arg e.g trace && instrument
+## value_next_arg - is the opton's value passed as next argument in command line?
+##
+## At least short_name or name needs to be in place for an argument descriptor to be valid.
+
+jvmv8.option.help = {                       \
+    name="-help",                             \
+    short_name="-h",                          \
+    desc="Print help for command line flags." \
+}
+
+jvmv8.option.xhelp = {                               \
+    name="-xhelp",                                     \
+    is_undocumented=true,                              \
+    desc="Print extended help for command line flags." \
+}
+
+jvmv8.option.classpath ={                                   \
+    name="-classpath",                                        \
+    short_name="-cp",                                         \
+    desc="-cp path. Specify where to find user class files.", \
+    value_next_arg=true,                                      \
+    type=String                                               \
+}
+
+jvmv8.option.d = {                                             \

+string.script.error=\

+

+
+engine.info=\
+    Language {0} {1} implementation "{2}" {3}
+
+encoding.unsupported=\
+    encoding {0} is not supported
+
+main.usage=\
+Usage: {0} [options] [arguments...]\n\
+\n\
+where [options] include:\n\
+\  \-classpath <path>    Specify where to find user class files \n\
+\  \-cp <path>           Specify where to find user class files \n\
+\  \-D<name>=<value>     Set a system property \n\
+\  \-J<flag>             Pass <flag> directly to the runtime system \n\
+\  \-l <language>        Use specified scripting language \n\
+\  \-e <script>          Evaluate given script \n\
+\  \-encoding <encoding> Specify character encoding used by script files \n\
+\  \-f <script file>     Evaluate given script file \n\
+\  \-f -                 Interactive mode, read script from standard input \n\
+\  \                     If this is used, this should be the last -f option \n\
+\  \-help                Print this usage message and exit \n\
+\  \-?                   Print this usage message and exit \n\
+\  \-q                   List all scripting engines available and exit \n\
+\  \n\
+If [arguments..] are present and if no -e or -f option is used, then first\n\
+argument is script file and the rest of the arguments, if any, are passed\n\
+as script arguments. If [arguments..] and -e or -f option is used, then all\n\
+[arguments..] are passed as script arguments. If [arguments..], -e, -f are\n\
+missing, then interactive mode is used.
+

@@ -0,0 +1,234 @@
+/*
+ * Copyright (c) 2018, 2025, Oracle and/or its affiliates. All rights reserved.
+ * DO NOT ALTER OR REMOVE COPYRIGHT NOTICES OR THIS FILE HEADER.
+ *
+ * This code is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it
+ * under the terms of the GNU General Public License version 2 only, as
+ * published by the Free Software Foundation.
+ *
+ * This code is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, but WITHOUT
+ * ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of MERCHANTABILITY or
+ * FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU General Public License
+ * version 2 for more details (a copy is included in the LICENSE file that
+ * accompanied this code).
+ *
+ * You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License version
+ * 2 along with this work; if not, write to the Free Software Foundation,
+ * Inc., 51 Franklin St, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA 02110-1301 USA.
+ *
+ * Please contact Oracle, 500 Oracle Parkway, Redwood Shores, CA 94065 USA
+ * or visit www.oracle.com if you need additional information or have any
+ * questions.
+ */
+
+
+/**
+ * Tests for conversion of JavaScript arrays to Java arrays and the other
+ * way round. Also generally useful as a JavaScript-to-Java type conversion
+ * test.
+ */
+
+var x; // used for undefined
+var testCount = 0;
+
+function printStackTrace() {
+    print(new Error().stack);
+}
+
+function testF(inputValue, type, testFn) {
+  var x = Java.to([inputValue], type + "[]")[0];
+  if(!testFn(x)) {
+    printStackTrace()
+    throw ("unexpected value: " + x)
+  }
+  ++testCount;
+}
+
+function test(inputValue, type, expectedValue) {
+  testF(inputValue, type, function(x) { return x === expectedValue })
+}
+
+function testNaN(inputValue, type) {
+  testF(inputValue, type, isNaN)
+}
+
+// Those labeled "Correct?" are not clearly correct conversions. Those
+// labeled "TypeError maybe?" could actually throw a TypeError, or only
+// throw a TypeError when in strict mode.
+// The case of ("false", "boolean") => true is particularly amusing.
+
+test(x, "int", 0) // Correct? TypeError maybe?
+test(null, "int", 0) // Correct? TypeError maybe?
+test(1234, "int", 1234)
+test("1234", "int", 1234)
+test("1234.49", "int", 1234)
+test("1234.51", "int", 1234) // truncates, not rounds
+test(true, "int", 1)
+test(false, "int", 0)
+test("foo", "int", 0) // Correct? TypeError maybe?
+
+test(x, "boolean", false) // Correct? TypeError maybe?
+test(null, "boolean", false) // Correct? TypeError maybe?
+test(0, "boolean", false)
+test(1234, "boolean", true)
+test("foo", "boolean", true)
+test("", "boolean", false)
+test("false", "boolean", true) // Correct? false maybe?
+
+test(x, "java.lang.String", "undefined") // Correct? TypeError maybe?
+test(null, "java.lang.String", null)
+test(1234, "java.lang.String", "1234")
+test(1234.5, "java.lang.String", "1234.5")
+test(true, "java.lang.String", "true")
+test(false, "java.lang.String", "false")
+
+test(x, "java.lang.Integer", null) // Correct? TypeError maybe?
+test(null, "java.lang.Integer", null)
+test(1234, "java.lang.Integer", 1234)
+test("1234", "java.lang.Integer", 1234)
+test("1234.49", "java.lang.Integer", 1234)
+test("1234.51", "java.lang.Integer", 1234) // truncates, not rounds
+test(true, "java.lang.Integer", 1)
+test(false, "java.lang.Integer", 0)
+test("foo", "java.lang.Integer", 0) // Correct? TypeError maybe?
+
+test(x, "java.lang.Boolean", null) // Correct? TypeError maybe?
+test(null, "java.lang.Boolean", null)
+test(0, "java.lang.Boolean", false)
+test(1234, "java.lang.Boolean", true)
+test("foo", "java.lang.Boolean", true)
+test("", "java.lang.Boolean", false)
+test("false", "java.lang.Boolean", true) // Correct? false maybe?
+
+testNaN(x, "double")
+test(null, "double", 0)
+test(1234, "double", 1234)
+test("1234", "double", 1234)
+test("1234.5", "double", 1234.5)
+test(true, "double", 1)
+test(false, "double", 0)
+testNaN("foo", "double")
+
+testNaN(x, "java.lang.Double")
+test(null, "java.lang.Double", null)
+test(1234, "java.lang.Double", 1234)
+test("1234", "java.lang.Double", 1234)
+test("1234.5", "java.lang.Double", 1234.5)
+test(true, "java.lang.Double", 1)
+test(false, "java.lang.Double", 0)
+testNaN("foo", "java.lang.Double")
+
+test({ valueOf: function() { return 42; } }, "int", 42)
+test({ valueOf: function() { return "42"; } }, "int", 42)
+// If there's no valueOf, toString is used
+test({ toString: function() { return "42"; } }, "int", 42)
+// For numbers, valueOf takes precedence over toString
+test({ valueOf: function() { return "42"; },  toString: function() { return "43"; } }, "int", 42)
+
+test({ toString: function() { return "foo"; } }, "java.lang.String", "foo")
+// Yep, even if we have valueOf, toString from prototype takes precedence
+test({ valueOf: function() { return 42; } }, "java.lang.String", "[object Object]")
+// Converting to string, toString takes precedence over valueOf
+test({ valueOf: function() { return "42"; },  toString: function() { return "43"; } }, "java.lang.String", "43")
+
+function assertCanConvert(sourceType, targetType) {
+  Java.to([new (Java.type(sourceType))()], targetType + "[]")
+  ++testCount;
+}
+
+function assertCantConvert(sourceType, targetType) {
+  try {
+    Java.to([new (Java.type(sourceType))()], targetType + "[]")
+    throw "no TypeError encountered"
+  } catch(e) {
+      if(!(e instanceof TypeError)) {
+        printStackTrace()
+        throw e;
+      }
+      ++testCount;
+  }
+}
+
+// Arbitrary POJOs to JS Primitive type should work
+assertCanConvert("java.util.BitSet", "int")
+assertCanConvert("java.util.BitSet", "double")
+assertCanConvert("java.util.BitSet", "long")
+assertCanConvert("java.util.BitSet", "boolean")
+assertCanConvert("java.util.BitSet", "java.lang.String")
+
+// Arbitrary POJOs can't be converted to Java values
+assertCantConvert("java.util.BitSet", "java.lang.Double")
+assertCantConvert("java.util.BitSet", "java.lang.Long")
+
+/***************************************************************************
+ * Now testing the other way round - Java arrays & collections to JavaScript
+ **************************************************************************/
+
+function assert(x) {
+  if(!x) {
+    printStackTrace()
+    throw "Assertion failed"
+  }
+  ++testCount;
+}
+
+var intArray = new (Java.type("int[]"))(3)
+intArray[0] = 1234;
+intArray[1] = 42;
+intArray[2] = 5;
+var jsIntArray = Java.from(intArray)
+assert(jsIntArray instanceof Array);
+assert(jsIntArray[0] === 1234);
+assert(jsIntArray[1] === 42);
+assert(jsIntArray[2] === 5);
+
+// The arrays are copies, they don't reflect each other
+intArray[2] = 6;
+assert(jsIntArray[2] === 5);
+jsIntArray[2] = 7;
+assert(intArray[2] === 6);
+
+var byteArray = new (Java.type("byte[]"))(2)
+byteArray[0] = -128;
+byteArray[1] = 127;
+var jsByteArray = Java.from(byteArray)
+assert(jsByteArray instanceof Array);
+assert(jsByteArray[0] === -128);
+assert(jsByteArray[1] === 127);
+
+var shortArray = new (Java.type("short[]"))(2)
+shortArray[0] = -32768;
+shortArray[1] = 32767;
+var jsShortArray = Java.from(shortArray)
+assert(jsShortArray instanceof Array);
+assert(jsShortArray[0] === -32768);
+assert(jsShortArray[1] === 32767);
+
+var floatArray = new (Java.type("float[]"))(2)
+floatArray[0] = java.lang.Float.MIN_VALUE;
+floatArray[1] = java.lang.Float.MAX_VALUE;
+var jsFloatArray = Java.from(floatArray)
+assert(jsFloatArray instanceof Array);
+assert(jsFloatArray[0] == java.lang.Float.MIN_VALUE);
+assert(jsFloatArray[1] == java.lang.Float.MAX_VALUE);
+
+var charArray = new (Java.type("char[]"))(3)
+charArray[0] = "a";
+charArray[1] = "b";
+charArray[2] = "1";
+var jsCharArray = Java.from(charArray)
+assert(jsCharArray instanceof Array);
+assert(jsCharArray[0] === 'a');
+assert(jsCharArray[1] === 'b');
+assert(jsCharArray[2] === '1');
+
+
+var booleanArray = new (Java.type("boolean[]"))(2)
+booleanArray[0] = true;
+booleanArray[1] = false;
+var jsBooleanArray = Java.from(booleanArray)
+assert(jsBooleanArray instanceof Array);
+assert(jsBooleanArray[0] === true);
+assert(jsBooleanArray[1] === false);
+
+print(testCount + " tests completed ok")

+ * or visit www.oracle.com if you need additional information or have any
+ * questions.
+ */
+
+
+var a = ['a', 'b', 'c', 'd']
+
+var l = Java.to(a, java.util.List)
+assertTrue(l instanceof java.util.List)
+assertTrue(l instanceof java.util.Deque)
+
+assertEquals(l[0], 'a')
+assertEquals(l[1], 'b')
+assertEquals(l[2], 'c')
+assertEquals(l[3], 'd')
+
+assertEquals(l.size(), 4)
+
+l.push('x')
+assertEquals(a.toString(), "x,a,b,c,d")
+
+l.addLast('y')
+assertEquals(a.toString(), "x,a,b,c,d,y")
+
+assertEquals(l.pop().toString(), "x")
+assertEquals(l.removeLast().toString(), "y")
+assertEquals(a.toString(), "a,b,c,d")
+
+l.add('e')
+l.add(5, 'f')
+assertEquals(a.toString(), "a,b,c,d,e,f");
+
+l.add(0, 'z')
+assertEquals(a.toString(), "z,a,b,c,d,e,f");
+
+l.add(2, 'x')
+assertEquals(a.toString(), "z,a,x,b,c,d,e,f");
+
+l[7] = 'g'
+assertEquals(a.toString(), "z,a,x,b,c,d,e,g");
+
+const IOBE = java.lang.IndexOutOfBoundsException;
+
+try { l.add(15, '') } catch(e) { assertTrue(e.javaException instanceof IOBE) }
+try { l.remove(15) } catch(e) { assertTrue(e.javaException instanceof IOBE) }
+try { l.add(-1, '') } catch(e) { assertTrue(e.javaException instanceof IOBE) }
+try { l.remove(-1) } catch(e) { assertTrue(e.javaException instanceof IOBE) }
+
+l.remove(7)
+l.remove(2)
+l.remove(0)
+assertEquals(a.toString(), "a,b,c,d,e");
+
+assertEquals(l.peek().toString(), "a")
+assertEquals(l.peekFirst().toString(), "a")
+assertEquals(l.peekLast().toString(), "e")
+
+assertEquals(l.element().toString(), "a")
+assertEquals(l.getFirst().toString(), "a")
+assertEquals(l.getLast().toString(), "e")
+
+l.offer('1')
+l.offerFirst('2')
+l.offerLast('3')
+assertEquals(a.toString(), "2,a,b,c,d,e,1,3")
+
+a = ['1', '2', 'x', '3', '4', 'x', '5', '6', 'x', '7', '8']
+assertEquals(a.toString(), "1,2,x,3,4,x,5,6,x,7,8")
+
+var l = Java.to(a, java.util.List)
+l.removeFirstOccurrence('x')
+assertEquals(a.toString(), "1,2,3,4,x,5,6,x,7,8")
+l.removeLastOccurrence('x')
+assertEquals(a.toString(), "1,2,3,4,x,5,6,7,8")
+
+var empty = Java.to([], java.util.List)
+const NSEE = java.util.NoSuchElementException;
+
+try { empty.pop() } catch(e) { assertTrue(e.javaException instanceof NSEE) }
+try { empty.removeFirst() } catch(e) { assertTrue(e.javaException instanceof NSEE) }
+try { empty.removeLast() } catch(e) { assertTrue(e.javaException instanceof NSEE) }
+
+try { empty.element() } catch(e) { assertTrue(e.javaException instanceof NSEE) }
+try { empty.getFirst() } catch(e) { assertTrue(e.javaException instanceof NSEE) }
+try { empty.getLast() } catch(e) { assertTrue(e.javaException instanceof NSEE) }
+
+assertTrue(empty.peek() === null)
+assertTrue(empty.peekFirst() === null)
+assertTrue(empty.peekLast() === null)
+
+// arguments object to Java List
+function func() {
+   return Java.to(arguments, java.util.List);
+}
+
+var l = func("hello", "world");
+assertTrue(l instanceof java.util.List);
+assertTrue(l.size() == 2);
+assertEquals(l.get(0), "hello");
+assertEquals(l.get(1), "world");

+

+ * questions.

+
+
+// Simple password generator using SecureRandom + stream API
+
+const LENGTH = 8;
+
+// Java types used
+const Collectors = Java.type("java.util.stream.Collectors");
+const IntFunction = Java.type("java.util.function.IntFunction");
+const SecureRandom = Java.type("java.security.SecureRandom");
+
+// allowed password chars
+const chars =
+    "!@#$%ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789";
+
+// generate password
+const password = new SecureRandom().
+    ints(LENGTH, 0, chars.length).
+    mapToObj(new IntFunction({ apply: i => chars[i] })).
+    collect(Collectors.joining(""));
+
+assertTrue(typeof password === 'string');
+assertTrue(password.length === LENGTH);
+
